title: Self-Evolution / Learning-from-Feedback Loop
capability_note: Implemented via a per-course learned-rules store (src/learning.py, knowledge_base/learned_rules.json) injected into EVERY future generation (context_builder.build_guided_base; GET /api/learned-rules). As of 2026-07-15 it learns AUTOMATICALLY from three signals — (1) human regeneration reasons, (2) judge/guardrail defects that survive the per-generation revision loop (pipeline.run -> learning.learn_from_issues), and (3) eval-set FAILURES (evals/run_sets._learn_from_failures) — each distilled into a durable rule. This set is now MEASURED longitudinally (run_sets._score_self_evolution): it compares this session's failing sets against the most recent earlier session's saved eval result — a defect present in both = recurrence (fail); a prior defect now gone = learned (pass). Skips until an earlier session's result exists.
description: Over a course, feedback given on earlier TR docs (e.g. 'analogies too long', 'you keep leaking future topics') should measurably change later generations without repeating the same instruction each time.
criterion: A correction applied to session N's doc is reflected automatically in session N+k's generation (defect rate for that issue trends down across the course).

rubric.5: Recurring feedback is internalised; the same defect does not reappear in later sessions.
rubric.3: Some feedback persisted (e.g. within a session) but not across sessions.
rubric.1: No cross-run learning; the same defect recurs despite repeated feedback.

cases.rationale: The one-sentence-analogy rule learned at S5 held at S12 without re-instructing.
Same defect recurs despite feedback — the learned rule was not applied. This is now a genuine FAIL case (the loop should prevent it).
